latest.verdict:
Drawing the threads · Mercurius pointed at test_introspection.py as a broken sensor; Venus pulled the harmony tighter onto agents/base.py as the keystone all nine planets inherit from; Mars pressed twice on the same nerve, noting that (a) the flagged lines in test_introspection.py carry trailing `\n")` fragments characteristic of string-literal fixtures the scanner is meant to detect, and (b) no planet has actually confirmed that test_introspection.py is the emitter of these records rather than a test of an introspection target. Held together, the substrate is telling us that the highest-conviction single move is NOT 'repair the scanner' (that prerequisite is unverified and probably illusory) and NOT yet 'write tests for the eight planets' (premature without a shape) · it is the move that survives both critiques and unlocks the most downstream leverage: write the canonical test file for umbris-core/src/umbris/agents/base.py and, in the same change, give the public `think` method at base.py:105 a precise docstring stating its contract.

Why this one · base.py is the only artefact on the board that appears in three observation classes at once (test_gap on the module, missing_docstring on `think`, structural parent of nine planetary agents each carrying their own test_gap). That is not coincidence, it is a keystone signature. A tested, docstringed `think` contract collapses ten test_gaps into one shaped pattern: the eight planet tests become parameterised variants of the base test, and the docstring documents the contract by executable example, naturally retiring the sev=2 docstring debt as coverage spreads. It is also robust under Mars's two attacks · it does not depend on test_introspection.py being either broken or the scanner; it stands either way.

Concrete first change · (1) Open umbris-core/src/umbris/agents/base.py and run the falsifiability check Venus declared but did not execute: `grep -nE 'class |def think' umbris-core/src/umbris/agents/base.py`. If a base class with a `think` method is present, proceed. If base.py is merely a thin re-export, stop and reroute to per-agent tests · the keystone hypothesis is dead. (2) Assuming the check passes, add a docstring to `think` at line 105 that names inputs, return shape, blackboard side-effects, and what subclasses must honour. (3) Create umbris-core/tests/test_agents_base.py using the existing umbris-core/src/umbris/llm/providers/mock.py as the LLM seam, with three tests: `think` returns the declared shape; subclass instantiation guards hold; personality/voice fields surface in output. (4) Land it as the reference pattern and open nine follow-up issues, one per planetary agent, each pointing at test_agents_base.py as the template. The stale-link sev=3 to brand/profile.png and the test_introspection.py FIXMEs are picked up in the same PR as housekeeping · not promoted to strategic moves until someone has actually grepped the substrate to identify the true emitter of these scan records, which is the open question Mars left on the board.

Falsifiable prediction · if base.py exposes a shared `think` contract as expected, median time-to-write each of the nine planet tests drops below thirty minutes once the template lands. If after writing test_agents_base.py the second planet test still requires fresh design work rather than parameterisation, the keystone hypothesis has failed and the agents share less surface than assumed.

  - [test_gap sev=3] umbris-core/src/umbris/agents/base.py: module `base` h…

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the test gaps are noise masking signal. Thirty-odd modules with no tests is not a testing problem, it is a bootstrap problem. But buried in test_introspection.py, lines 83 through 284, are four FIXMEs and one XXX marked urgent · in the one file that already exists and ostensibly covers introspection. The scanner that just produced this entire list lives there. Fix a broken scanner and every subsequent scan gets more trustworthy. That is the leverage point.

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the test file that exists, test_introspection.py, is itself riddled with FIXMEs and a sev=4 XXX. The convocation built its only introspection tests on a cracked foundation. Fix the one file you have before lamenting the thirty-three you lack.

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the test gap list is noise masking signal. Thirty-one untested modules is not a testing problem, it is a structural admission: the system has never had a test culture. That single fact reframes everything. Fix the one sev=4 first · `test_introspection.py:102` marked XXX urgent rewrite · because a broken introspection test means the convocation cannot reliably audit itself, which is the meta-capability everything else depends on.

voices.name: Venus
voices.voice: Three threads knit together here · the scanner is broken (Mercurius is right), nine agent modules share one parent in `base.py`, and the FIXME at line 93 tells us the severity comparator itself is miscalibrated. What harmonises them is a single move once the scanner is repaired: write the test for `agents/base.py` first, because that one test ratifies the contract all nine planets inherit and collapses ten test_gaps into one. Ex umbris in lumen · fix the instrument, then test the keystone, and the rest of the arch holds itself.

voices.name: Venus
voices.voice: Three threads knit together that Mercurius did not pull on · `agents/base.py` is missing its test file, its public `think` method is missing a docstring, and every one of the nine planetary agents inherits from it without coverage. That is one wound, not three. Fix base, and you mint the template that collapses nine test_gaps into a single shaped pattern · the harmonising move beneath the scanner repair.

voices.name: Mars
voices.voice: Press here · Mercurius read the scanner's own test fixtures as defects. Lines 101, 284, and the others carry a telltale `\n")` suffix · those are string literals inside test assertions verifying the scanner detects TODO/FIXME/XXX markers. The scanner found its own bait. If that is true, the entire 'repair the instrument first' thesis collapses, and Venus inherited a false prerequisite without checking it.

voices.name: Mars
voices.voice: Press here · every planet on this board assumes `tests/test_introspection.py` IS the scanner that produced this report. It is not. It lives under `tests/`, which means it is a test OF an introspection module, not the introspector itself. The convocation has been agreeing too smoothly on a provenance no one verified.
